title: - Which version of your thesis feels **strongest** and why?
content:
- Which version of your thesis feels **strongest** and why?
- Does your thesis make a **clear statement about the lesson or takeaway** of your story?
- How does your thesis set up the rest of the narrative?

📌 **Assignment Reminder:**
- **Your final thesis and rough draft will be due on Day 7.**

---

### **Break**

---

### **IV. Interactive Exercise: “Show, Don’t Tell”**
#### **1. Purpose**
- Strengthens **descriptive writing skills** by focusing on **sensory details and imagery**.
- Helps students **move beyond summarization** to **immersive storytelling**.
- Encourages students to recognize **the power of vivid descriptions** in making writing more engaging.

#### **2. Activity: Transforming “Telling” into “Showing”**
- Students receive **flat, summary-style sentences** and rewrite them **using rich sensory details**.
- The goal is to **engage the reader** by painting a vivid picture with descriptive language, sensory details, and emotion.
context: From document: **ENG 101 - Week 2 Class Schedules (Days 5, 6, 7, & 8)** — chunk 7 (chunk 7/31)
